Name: Thomas W. Peterson
Bio:
Thomas Peterson is a member of the Utah House of Representatives, representing the Utah House's District 1, which covers both Box Elder County and Cache County. and he assumed office in 2022.

Thomas Peterson graduated from Box Elder High School in 1996. He has served as the chairman of the Uniform Building Code Commission and on the board of directors of the International Code Council. Bio: Katy began her legislative service in 2023, and has since then represented Utah's House district 11. House District 11 consists of Davis County and Weber County. Before her political career, Katy worked as a registered nurse, bringing her healthcare perspective to her legislative role. She holds a degree in nursing from the University of Wisconsin Milwaukee, which equipped her with the knowledge and skills necessary for her work as a registered nurse. Hall is passionate about outdoor activities

Name: Mike Schultz
Bio: Mike Schultz has represented Utah's 12th House District since 2015 and currently serves as Speaker of the House. He has championed policies like increasing funding for Utah students, conserving Utah's water supply, and investing in transportation infrastructure all while balancing the budget and cutting taxes. Bio:
Paul Cutler began his legislative service in 2023. Since then, he has represented Utah's House District 18, which consists of Davis County.

He holds a Bachelor of Science in Electrical Engineering from the University of Utah and an MBA from Brigham Young University. Professionally, Cutler has extensive experience in technology consulting and business, and currently works as an Engineering Director for a global technology company.

Bio: Mike Petersen, State Representative for Utah House's District 2, began his legislative service in 2021. Name: Melissa G. Ballard
Bio: She and her husband, Craig Ballard, are the parents of 6 children ages 21-31. She received a Masters Degree in Piano Performance University of Utah '94, performed in NYC Carnegie Hall 2023, and recorded an album with Centaur Records receiving critics' choice awards in 2020.

Bio:

Anthony's career began in local government, where he held a variety of roles that provided valuable experience in policy analysis, community engagement, and leadership. Cal and his wife, Elizabeth, live in Draper with their three children. A graduate of Brigham Young University, Cal began his career on Wall Street with Credit Suisse doing Mergers & Acquisitions before returning to Utah to raise his family and build his career in private equity and finance.

Bio:

In addition to his roles as a husband, father, volunteer firefighter, EMT, and avid sportsman, Representative Snider holds another important title-- House Majority Leader.

Name: Stephanie Gricius
Bio: Representative Stephanie Gricius is a dedicated public servant, entrepreneur, and longtime advocate for her community. A longtime resident of Eagle Mountain, Stephanie has been deeply involved in service from a young age through roles in city government, the Republican Party, and nonprofit work. Name: Leah Hansen
Bio:
Representative Leah Hansen was appointed to the Utah House in 2025. Representative Hansen represents District 51, which encompasses Saratoga Springs and portions of Eagle Mountain and Lehi.

Leah attended Weber State University before prioritizing family to raise her children. She brings a strong desire to learn, listen and collaborate with colleagues, and is eager to grow through mentorship and community engagement. Name: A. Cory Maloy
Bio:
Representative Maloy began his legislative service in 2017. He currently represents the Utah House's 52nd district which consists of Utah County.

Cory attended Ricks College in Idaho for an associate's degree in business, followed by Brigham Young University for a Bachelor of Arts in Communications with a public relations focus. At BYU, he excelled as a reporter and editor at the Daily Universe, winning the Columbia Scholastic Press Association Gold Circle Award for news writing.

Bio: Representative Kay Christofferson has served in the Utah State Legislature since 2013. Representative Christofferson earned his B.S., Civil Engineering, from Brigham Young University. He worked in the heavy Civil Construction Industry as a Vice President and President of several companies. He represents the family values that he grew up with in Lehi. With his wonderful wife Suzanne, they have seven children and sixteen grandchildren.

Bio:

Bio:

Born in Idaho, Rep. Peterson soon found Utah to be home shortly after his LDS mission in Colombia while attending Brigham Young University. His career experience includes working as the executive vice president of Rocky Mountain University of Health Professions and a tenured professor of communications and associate vice president at Utah Valley University. Her family has farmed in the Leland, Spanish Fork, and Salem area for generations, and agriculture has remained a central part of her life through 4-H, rodeo, and community involvement. Today, she and her husband continue to operate Seven Bar Farming while raising their family in Spanish Fork.

After returning, He moved to Cedar City to attend SUSC (now known as SUU). He and his wife Stacia have 7 children.

Bio:

He is committed to helping his neighbors maintain their freedom and liberty.

Name: Jake Sawyer
Bio:

With a strong background in leadership, Jake is committed to addressing Utah's housing challenges and serving the needs of his community. He brings a results-driven approach to the state legislature, leveraging over 14 years of experience in executive leadership and team management.

Previously she worked as the Legislative Policy Director for Gov. Spencer Cox and as the Deputy Education Policy Advisor for Governor Gary Herbert.

Name: Karianne Lisonbee
Bio:
Representative Karianne Lisonbee's legislative efforts have spanned a range of issues, from healthcare and tax reform to educational opportunity and economic development. Representative Lisonbee passed significant legislation protecting victims and making Utah's communities and schools safer through increasing accountability and transparency in the criminal justice system. Her efforts have been instrumental in advancing Utah's prosperity.

Name: Trevor Lee

He graduated from Davis High School and holds a degree in technical sales with a minor in Spanish from Weber State University. His professional background includes owning a wealth management company and consulting for businesses. This diverse experience has equipped him with a practical understanding of the financial challenges faced by small businesses

Following his medical education, he completed a General Surgery Internship at Washington Medical Center and a Residency in Otolaryngology, Head and Neck Surgery at Georgetown University Hospital.

He is a proud graduate of Grantsville High School. After graduation, Rep. Bolinder started several small businesses, including Bolinder Resources, a sand and gravel/mining company based in Tooele, Utah. He works in these businesses when not in session.

Bio: Representative Cheryl Acton was born and raised in Kansas before moving to Arizona for her senior year of high school. She lived in northern Denmark for a year as a Rotary Youth Ambassador, then arrived in Utah to begin her freshman year at BYU. Awed by Utah's scenic beauty and its wonderful people, she takes enormous pride in her adopted home state. Name: Doug Fiefia
Bio:
Doug Fiefia represents Utah's House District 48, which consists of Herriman and Riverton.

Doug is a first generation American, whose parents came from Tonga to the US in the late 70s to achieve the American Dream. He grew up in South Jordan, Utah where he led the Bingham High School football team to the first state championship in over 60 years. Bio:
